summary: Decompiled and improved matching for the Rules Plus menu implementation in src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c and refined its header data layout. The PR replaced several placeholder/commented functions with C implementations for input handling, animation updates, description text, rule persistence, menu construction, and time-limit rendering. decomp.dev reported 3 new full matches, 7 improved unmatched items, +2132 matched bytes, and overall matched code movement from 61.45% to 61.50%. Evidence for author intent is weak because the PR body only says "Tracking PR as usual" and there were no human review comments.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: added includes, constants, helpers, and implementations for Rules Plus menu functions including fn_8023201C, mn_802324E4, mn_80232660, mn_802327A4, mn_80232D4C, fn_80232F44, and mn_80233218.

Header/data-layout updates: added RulePlusOption enum, converted MenuRulesPlusData rule_values to a named-field/indexable union, and changed mn_803ED1D0_t animation fields into arrays with selected/unselected semantics.

what_changed: Added real C for fn_8023201C, the Rules Plus input handler. It reads buttons via mn_80229624(4), stores mn_804A04F0.buttons, handles A/Start/B/D-pad directions, saves rule values, enters stage select via mnStageSw_80237410, and wraps/skips invisible menu options.
Added static inline mnRulePlus_SaveRules to copy MenuRulesPlusData rule_values into gmMainLib_8015CC34 fields: stock_time_limit, friendly_fire, pause, score_display, and unk_xc/sd_penalty.

Added static inline mnRulePlus_IsOptionVisible to centralize Rules Plus visibility rules: option 1 hidden for gm_801A4310() == 0x1B, option 3 gated by gmMainLib_8015EE0C(), option 5 gated by gmMainLib_8015EE44(), otherwise visible.
Implemented mn_802324E4 for time-limit display animation, including the zero-time special case that hides digit JObjs and the nonzero case that animates tens/ones and related number parts.
Implemented mn_80232660 and mn_80232660_inline. The bot report lists mn_80232660 as a new 100% match worth 324 bytes.

Implemented fn_80232F44 as a new 100% match worth 724 bytes according to decomp.dev. It manages Rules Plus per-frame state transitions, animation completion, visual/text refreshes, selection/value changes, and writes changed rules back to gmMainLib_8015CC34.
Replaced a large commented pseudocode block for mn_80233218 with a substantial C implementation. decomp.dev reports mn_80233218 improved from 0.00% to 76.29% and +1541 bytes.
Improved partial matches for mn_802327A4, mn_80232D4C, mn_802324E4, .data, .sdata2, and .sbss per the automated report.
Filled previously zeroed AnimLoopSettings arrays mn_803ED270 and mn_803ED294 with concrete start/end/loop values, and reshaped mn_803ED1D0 initializers to match the revised struct layout.
Changed extern MenuKindData* mn_803EB6B0 to extern MenuKindData mn_803EB6B0[] and added extern f32 mn_804D6BE4.
Added a JObjIndices union with s32 packed and u8 idx[4], then used static packed initializers mn_804DBE40 and mn_804DBE48 for byte-indexed JObj loops.
Added needed includes for placeholder/debug/gobjplink/sislib/gm_1A3F/lb_00F9/lbaudio_ax/mnmainrule/mnstagesw/dolphin OS so the decompiled code can call the real HSD, gm, lb, audio, and menu APIs.
smart_moves: Factored repeated global-rule writes into mnRulePlus_SaveRules, reducing duplicated assignments while preserving exact field mapping to gmMainLib_8015CC34.
Factored option hiding logic into mnRulePlus_IsOptionVisible and reused it in D-pad navigation; this documents the mode-specific menu behavior and makes wraparound selection logic clearer.
Used a union for MenuRulesPlusData.rule_values so code can use descriptive fields for rule semantics and values[] for generic indexed option handling.
Used JObjIndices to preserve packed 0x02030506 data while allowing byte-wise idx[] iteration, a useful pattern for matching data that is stored as a word but consumed as small indices.
Converted mn_803ED1D0_t x64/x7C fields into two-element AnimLoopSettings arrays with comments for selected/unselected non-stage and stage cases, matching how code indexes by selection state.

Kept the original HSD object flow explicit: GObj_Create, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_GObj_SetupProc, HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, user data allocation, and child JObj extraction via lb_80011E24.
Used the automated decomp.dev result as validation: 3 new full matches and 7 improved items, with .data/.sdata2/.sbss improvements indicating the data-layout changes were not just cosmetic.
decomp_lessons: For menu decompilation, separate persistent menu user_data, live global menu state mn_804A04F0, and global game rules. This PR copies from gmMainLib_8015CC34 into MenuRulesPlusData at creation, then writes back on value changes or exits.
When rules/options are semantically named but also indexed by the UI, a union of named fields and an array can make both the code and matching easier.

Visibility predicates should be kept identical across input handling and rendering; otherwise hidden options can still be selected or animated. Here the same gm_801A4310/gmMainLib gates appear in navigation and display logic.

HSD_SisLib text descriptions need explicit lifetime management: free the old text with HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4, clear the pointer, create new text with HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, set font_size, then load the message id with HSD_SisLib_803A6368.
Animation frame tables are part of matching. Filling mn_803ED270/mn_803ED294 and structuring mn_803ED1D0_t around how the code indexes frames improved .data and .sdata2 matching.
Packed constants that appear as byte indices in loops may need a union or equivalent layout trick rather than four separate literals.
Function progress can be meaningful even when not fully matched: mn_80233218, mn_802327A4, mn_80232D4C, and mn_802324E4 remained partial in the bot report but gained large matched-byte improvements.
naming_conventions: New enum names use the RULEPLUS_* prefix: RULEPLUS_TIME_LIMIT, RULEPLUS_FRIENDLY_FIRE, RULEPLUS_PAUSE, RULEPLUS_SCORE, RULEPLUS_SD_PENALTY, RULEPLUS_STAGE_SELECT, RULEPLUS_COUNT.
Local helper names use a readable mnRulePlus_* prefix rather than raw addresses: mnRulePlus_SaveRules and mnRulePlus_IsOptionVisible.
Unknown-address symbols remain in project style when not confidently named: fn_8023201C, fn_80232F44, mn_802324E4, mn_80232660, mn_802327A4, mn_80232D4C, mn_80233218.
Header comments document inferred semantics without overnaming all fields: x64[2] is noted as unselected/selected non-stage, and x7C[2] as unselected/selected stage.
MenuRulesPlusData keeps unknown fields such as x7, xC, and x34 where evidence is insufficient, while naming known concepts like menu_kind, hovered_selection, rule_values, state, and description.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in matched/near-matched functions, including PAD_STACK(0x30) in fn_8023201C, PAD_STACK(8) in mn_80232660, and PAD_STACK(0x18) in fn_80232F44.
Used inline helpers to reproduce call/inlining behavior while making repeated rule-save and animation-scan logic readable.
Preserved compiler-sensitive casts to u8/u16/s32 around hovered_selection, confirmed_selection, and option indices.
Initialized jobj_map[17] explicitly or via loops to support lb_8001204C child-JObj extraction in the same shape as the original code.
Modeled the switch/fallthrough behavior in mn_80232660: options 1 through 4 call mn_80232660_inline and fall through to return, while options 0 and 5 return directly.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT with the observed line value 0x3DF and message "Can't get user_data.\n" when allocating MenuRulesPlusData.
Used packed JObjIndices static data for mn_804DBE40 and mn_804DBE48 to satisfy both data matching and indexed JObj access.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
The only issue comment was an automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showing matched code 61.50% (+0.05%, +2132 bytes), 3 new matches, and 7 improved unmatched items.
follow_up_queries: mn_802327A4 mnruleplus selection animation remaining mismatch
mn_80232D4C Rules Plus description text state handling
mn_802324E4 time limit digit JObj indices mn_804DBE40 mn_804DBE48
